p.seg: Let all who shall see or hear this charter know that I, Simon of Lindsay, have given and granted and by my present charter made firm to Helen my daughter my land at ‘Hungerigge’ that I hold in the territory of Mow of Robert of Pollok and his wife Isabel, and a certain meadow within ‘Ederedesete’ that is called ‘Holemede’, which I hold of the lady Eschyna and her heirs; for her and her heirs to hold and to have of the said Robert and his heirs, and of the lady Eschyna and her heirs, free and quit of every service and aid and exaction; by annually returning thereupon to the said Robert and his heirs ten shillings, that is five shillings at Pentecost and five shillings at the feast of St Martin; and to the lady Eschyna and her heirs one red not moulted hawk at the Roxburgh Fair. And if it should happen that the said Helen has died by ill fortune without issue, I will that my daughter Eschina, her sister and heir, succeed her in the said land. And I have bestowed and entrusted the said land to Helen and her heirs as freely and quitely in all things and through all things as the charter of the said Robert and his wife Isabel, and the confirmation of the heirs of the lady Eschina of Mow composed thereupon, confer and bear witness.
